program_name: [uncertain] Cognitive Science for Human-Centric Technologies - COGTECH
campus: Padua (the programme is mainly held online, with Padua as the study location)

academic_requirements.summary: [uncertain] Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent. The official apply page says a minimum three-year undergraduate degree is required, but it also says that more information on required competencies will be available soon.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: [uncertain] A minimum three-year undergraduate degree or equivalent is required. More information on required competencies will be available soon.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: Applicants must verify country-based specific entry title and GPA requirements on the University's official entry-title requirements page.

language_requirements.required_level: B2 CEFR English or equivalent certificate.
accepted_proofs: Pearson Test of English General (PTE General)
Pearson Test of English Academic (PTE Academic)
exemption_rules: Applicants are exempt if they completed their previous education entirely in English.
Applicants are exempt if they are native English speakers from the countries listed on the official language-requirement page.
IMAT candidates for Medicine and Surgery are exempt under the University's central English-language rule.
language_requirements.deadline: If language-requirement documentation was not uploaded during pre-enrolment, the official admission-notice page says it may be emailed by 31 July.

application_deadline_eu.call_three: No third EU call was stated on the official programme apply page reviewed on 2026-06-04.
application_deadline_eu.notes: Applies to EU and EU-equated applicants, and to non-EU applicants residing in Italy. The official page also says applicants can apply only once per academic year.

application_deadline_non_eu.call_two: No second non-EU call was stated on the official programme apply page reviewed on 2026-06-04.
application_deadline_non_eu.call_three: No third non-EU call was stated on the official programme apply page reviewed on 2026-06-04.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Applies to non-EU applicants residing outside Italy. The official page also says applicants can apply only once per academic year.

required_documents.document_name: Valid passport or valid ID card
required_documents.notes: The official apply portal lists a valid passport or a valid ID card for EU or equivalent applicants.
Required only for non-EU applicants already residing in Italy.
The transcript must be official and written or translated into Italian or English.

required_documents.document_name: Upper secondary school certificate or academic qualification
required_documents.notes: Upload it if already obtained, and verify country-based entry-title requirements on the official University page.
Listed in the general official application document list.
Listed in the general official application document list.
Required unless the applicant qualifies for an exemption under the official language-requirement page.
The programme page states that a specific evaluation form is required to apply.
If admitted, applicants must later submit further original and translated/legalised qualification documents. No legalised documents are required at application stage.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No separate entry exam; admission follows application evaluation and a ranking list.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: Assessment follows the published non-EU and EU/EU-equated application calls for academic year 2026/2027.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: This is a limited-access programme, and places are assigned through a ranking list of admitted students.

source_quotes.quote: More information on required competencies will be available soon.
Call for non-EU students: 7 January 2026 – 7 March 2026
Call Two for EU and EU-equated students: 15 July 2026 – 28 August 2026
A specific Evaluation form is required to apply.

uncertainty_notes: The official programme page uses the singular name 'Cognitive Science for Human-Centric Technologies - COGTECH', while the official apply portal uses 'Cognitive Sciences for Human-Centric Technologies'.

The official apply page says that more information on required competencies will be available soon, so subject-specific academic requirements are not yet fully published on the reviewed official pages.
This is a new 2026/2027 programme, and the reviewed official sources were live on 2026-06-04.
